data.summary: VIS-10 closed: HerdrDriver fails closed at every pane/tab creation site (mandatory --workspace/--tab, reject on all 4 degraded paths, workspace id seeded into every paneshell), suite migrated green RED-first, live check proved herdr ignores the env so hole 3 is partially closed with herdr finding HF-43-01 filed.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
.../43-readable-worker-panes/43-01-DIAGNOSIS.md    | 214 +++++++++++++++++++++
 .../43-readable-worker-panes/43-01-SUMMARY.md      |  84 ++++++++
 src/drivers/herdr.ts                               |  62 ++++--
 tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts                        | 133 +++++++++++--
 4 files changed, 459 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)

✓ Four untargeted-placement paths + missing env seed reproduced RED against unfixed HEAD, literal output and HEAD sha committed in 43-01-DIAGNOSIS.md, no src touched yet: 43-01-DIAGNOSIS.md contains the unfixed HEAD sha 23f8a311b81cca062f97e02748a47d4b70e72418 and the literal vitest output showing exactly 5 failures: the four reject oracles (env unset, non-zero exit, unparseable stdout, no tab_id) each resolving instead of rejecting, plus the seed oracle (expected 2, got 0). Commit history shows the red-first test commit 5a75cc8 preceding the src fix 07c4cc5, corroborating the src-untouched sequencing.
✓ Fail-closed placement live: no slot()→untargeted-pane path, every tab create carries --workspace, every pane shell seeded, migrated suite green, env-honoring question answered with committed live transcript: src/drivers/herdr.ts now throws on env unset, non-zero tab create, unparseable stdout, and missing tab_id; --workspace is unconditional; both agent start sites carry --tab; both tabSlot and joinGroup seed the pane shell. The live transcript (workspace list, agent start with HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID=wK landing in focused wJ, probe pane closed) is committed under '## Live env-honoring check' with the answer: IGNORED. Suite migration is visible in the diff and SUMMARY records 822 passed.
✓ Operator-mandated oracle: slot() rejects on all four degraded paths, reproduced RED first with literal vitest output under a heading containing 'RED proof (VIS-10)': tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts adds the four reject tests (env unset, tabFails, tabGarbage, tabNoId fixtures); the diagnosis heading is exactly '## RED proof (VIS-10)' and shows each of the four resolving with an untargeted pane on unfixed HEAD (RED).
✓ 'fall back to untargeted placement' string gone from src/drivers/herdr.ts; agent start never issued without --tab: The diff deletes the '/* fall back to untargeted placement */' catch and the `target = tabId ? ... : ""` ternary; both agent start invocations (initial and DEFECT-01 reclaim retry) now hardcode `--tab ${shq(tabId)}` after fail-closed checks guarantee tabId is a non-empty string. The unreachable `if (!s.tabId) return s` degrade in groupSlot is deleted.
✓ Positive command-log assertion that every tab create carries --workspace, red-capable by mutation, vacuity trap avoided: The 'every tab create carries --workspace' test runs with env set and a succeeding stub, extracts every 'tab create ' line from the command log, asserts ≥2 exist and each contains '--workspace wTEST' — deleting the flag at the creation site reddens it even on the happy path. It is not a happy-path-only reject test.
✓ Workspace id seeded into EVERY established pane shell (tabSlot + joinGroup split) via pane run export, fail-closed on seed failure, mechanism not a prompt instruction: tabSlot issues `pane run <id> export HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID=<ws>` after the agent pane is addressable and throws on non-zero exit; joinGroup seeds the split pane and on failure reaps it and returns null, routing the member through tabSlot whose own seed is reject-on-failure — no code path hands out an unseeded pane. The seed-count oracle asserts exactly 2 seeds across a tabSlot member and a joinGroup split member. The mechanism is driver code, not prompt text.
✓ Suite migrated not weakened: legacy graceful-degrade test rewritten to assert rejection; DEFECT-01, VIS-04, SUP-01, width-law all stay green with valid tab payload + env set: The legacy 'unset → graceful degrade' test is rewritten to `rejects.toThrow(/workspace/i)`. The stub default now emits a valid tab payload ({tab:true} kept as no-op alias), a file-scoped beforeEach sets HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID=wTEST with restore, and the RED-proof run plus SUMMARY show DEFECT-01/VIS-04/SUP-01 tests green; no existing assertion was deleted, only strengthened (e.g. agent start now asserted with --tab).
✓ npm test zero-token (stubbed binary only), tsc --noEmit exit 0, coverage floors held, runtime deps exactly picomatch/yaml/zod: package.json is untouched by the diff so runtime deps are unchanged by construction; all new tests use the makeStub shell-script binary (zero tokens). SUMMARY records 822 passed / 1 skipped, tsc exit 0, and coverage exit 0 with all 9 per-glob floors held.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Non-blocking: `this.ws` is captured at construction, not at slot() — env set after driver construction but before slot() would still reject; harmless in practice since pickDriver runs after the daemon inherits env, and the fail direction is closed, not open.
- Non-blocking: in joinGroup a failed seed degrades to a per-slot tab instead of rejecting the slot outright; the fallback path itself seeds fail-closed, so no unseeded/untargeted pane can result — a defensible reading of the acceptance criterion, worth a one-line note if the operator wants strict rejection there too.

data.summary: VIS-09 item 2 closed: workersPerTab cap (default 3) overflows the cap+1'th concurrent worker to a WORKERS-2 tab via per-generation groupSlot with per-tab refcounted teardown(width law untouched), plus a per-attempt completion nonce pinned by a red-verified stale-trailer oracle; pane reuse deferred with its TUI-occupancy hazard recorded.

data.details:
3 commit(s):
.../43-readable-worker-panes/43-02-SUMMARY.md      | 107 +++++++++++++++++++++
 src/config/config.ts                               |   9 +-
 src/drivers/herdr.ts                               |  70 +++++++++-----
 src/drivers/index.ts                               |   7 +-

 tests/config/config.test.ts                        |  40 +++++++-
 tests/drivers/herdr.test.ts                        |  79 +++++++++++++++
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts                           |  63 ++++++++++++
 8 files changed, 363 insertions(+), 31 deletions(-)

✓ Worker panes per tab bounded via extended groupSlot seam; cap+1'th lands in WORKERS-2; per-tab refcounted teardown; operator knob default 3; no rightward split: herdr.ts groupSlot now holds generations with cap check (members.length < workersPerTab), newGeneration opens WORKERS-2 via a second tab create; closeGrouped refcounts per generation tab; config default 3 plumbed through pickDriver; grep of src/ finds zero `--direction right`.
✓ Completion nonce per-attempt, pinned by stale-trailer oracle; pane reuse NOT implemented, hazard recorded as reversible call with evidence: nonce/exitMarkerCmd/exitRe moved into the attempt-loop body; daemon.test.ts 'a retained prior-attempt trailer cannot complete a retry' models shared never-cleared buffer retention; SUMMARY records the TUI-occupancy hazard with source evidence as a reversible orchestrator ruling; no reuse code in the diff.
✓ TAB CAP + OVERFLOW: with workersPerTab=2 the third concurrent member lands in a new WORKERS-2 tab (second tab create, not a third split), asserted via stub command log, RED on unfixed HEAD; per-tab refcounted teardown; f31c781/DEFECT-01 regressions stay green: New herdr tests assert exactly TWO `tab create` (labels WORKERS + WORKERS-2), ONE `pane split`, s3.tabId in the second tab — on unfixed HEAD (single GroupEntry) three members share one tab, so the assertions redden; per-tab teardown test asserts each tab closes exactly once on its own last member; existing driver tests untouched and the full suite (834 tests) passes.
✓ Cap counts LIVE members: after a tab-1 member closes, the next member joins tab 1 via a split, not a new tab: Test 'the cap counts LIVE members' closes n1 then slots n3, asserting one tab create total and two splits with n3.tabId === w1:t1; closeGrouped prunes members[] so groupSlot's cap check sees only live members.
✓ visibility.workersPerTab config key: positive int, DEFAULT_CONFIG 3, commented configTemplate example, omitting overlays parse, zero/negative rejected, plumbed via pickDriver with driver never importing config: Schema adds z.number().int().positive(); DEFAULT_CONFIG.visibility.workersPerTab = 3; template carries a commented example; tests cover default round-trip, override, omission (deepMerge → 3), zero and negative rejection; pickDriver passes cfg.visibility.workersPerTab to both HerdrDriver construction sites; herdr.ts imports no config module.
✓ PER-ATTEMPT NONCE: nonce moves from run scope into the attempt loop, pinned by an oracle where a retained attempt-0 trailer is not accepted by attempt 1, reddening on any hoist: The three nonce lines now sit at the top of the attempt-loop body with a fresh randomBytes per attempt; the oracle drives two attempts through a retaining driver with delayed shared-buffer delivery and asserts attempt 1 completes on FRESH-A1 not STALE-A0 — a run-scoped nonce would let attempt 1's first poll match attempt 0's retained marker, so a hoist reddens it.
✓ Nonce move is pure scope-tightening: no call-site signature changes, no prompt.ts change, all existing daemon behavioral assertions survive unmodified: The diff moves only the three declarations; prompt.ts is untouched; the only pre-existing test edit anywhere is the DEFAULT_CONFIG.visibility toEqual migration (unrelated to the nonce); all 834 tests pass.
✓ Pane reuse not implemented, 43-02-SUMMARY.md carries 'Deferred: pane reuse' heading with TUI-occupancy hazard, new-driver-primitive rationale, keepPanes:attempt note; driver.slot( called exactly once inside the attempt loop: SUMMARY line 40 is '## Deferred: pane reuse' with the daemon.ts TUI-does-not-exit hazard, the herdr.ts:144 pane-run-into-live-shell proof, the new-primitive requirement, and the keepPanes:attempt reaping note; grep confirms `driver.slot(` appears exactly once in daemon.ts, inside the attempt loop.
✓ Width law untouched: no code path emits --direction right, asserted across every new cap/overflow command log: grep of src/ finds no `--direction right`; the cap/overflow, concurrent, and dedicated width-law tests each assert its absence in the stub command log.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit exits 0, coverage floors hold, runtime deps exactly picomatch/yaml/zod: Verified by execution in the worktree: tsc exited 0, npm test 834 passed (fake adapter only), npm run test:coverage exited 0 with all per-glob thresholds enforced, and package.json dependencies are exactly picomatch, yaml, zod.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Non-blocking: groupSlot only checks the LATEST generation for live-member room — after members leave an older still-open generation (e.g. gen 1 drops to 1/3 while WORKERS-2 exists and is full), the next member opens WORKERS-3 instead of rejoining gen 1's free slot. Still bounded and consistent with the acceptance oracle (which only exercises the single-generation case), but slightly more tabs than strictly needed under churn.
- Non-blocking: closeGrouped matches the member's generation by slot.tabId but then prunes members by name; a degraded (splitUnsupported) member returned via tabSlot carries no group so it can't hit this path — verified fine — but the tabId-based generation lookup silently no-ops if a slot object from before the overflow refactor lacked tabId (none exist in-tree).

✓ Threshold measured against a real agent TUI with raw per-width captures and machine-readable results.json carrying real-parser verdicts (or honest refusal); no src/ touched at the measurement stage: Four raw captures at descending widths (rect 111/56/28/27, tput 108/53/25/24) vendored under measurement/ with results.json carrying per-width parseOk verdicts computed by the real parser; the src/ change is the separately-licensed ruling required by later criteria, not part of the probe artifact.
✓ Every vendored verdict recomputed from raw bytes by the real parser; captures carry provenance (tput cols + CLI banner); exactly one ruling shipped with herdr.ts citing 43-MEASUREMENT.md: trailer-width.test.ts recomputes each capture's verdict with parseWorkerResult imported from src/adapters/prompt.ts; every capture file contains a TPUTCOLS line and the '2.1.207 (Claude Code)' banner; exactly one ruling (Grid licensed) shipped as SAFE_FLOOR_COLS + splitDirection() gated by runtime width, with herdr.ts comments citing 43-MEASUREMENT.md.
✓ Verdicts are DERIVED, not narrated: zero-token suite re-reads each capture, runs the REAL parseWorkerResult, asserts match with results.json: The 'DERIVED, not narrated' test reads each vendored capture, runs parseWorkerResult(raw, NONCE) with the real import (never a lookalike), and asserts recomputed.ok equals the recorded parseOk for every entry.
✗ Provenance pinned INSIDE the captures and asserted by the suite test against the CAPTURE FILES: (a) real tput cols line, (b) the agent CLI's own banner/version string: (a) is properly asserted (TPUTCOLS integer cross-checked against recorded cols). (b) is NOT: the test asserts only raw.toContain(e.cli) where e.cli='claude' — a lowercase string that matches the ECHOED COMMAND LINE ('claude --version'), not the banner '2.1.207 (Claude Code)' (case-sensitive mismatch: 'Claude' ≠ 'claude'). The version banner is recorded in results.json as cliVersion but never asserted against any capture. A synthetic echo transcript that types the command would satisfy this assertion — the gate lands on exactly 'a doc string they can type', the failure mode this criterion explicitly forbids. Plausible-but-wrong: the banner exists in the files but the required assertion does not gate on it.
✓ Exactly one decision section in 43-MEASUREMENT.md ('Grid licensed' or 'Grid refused') answering all three licensing conditions with measured numbers: One '## Grid licensed' heading; condition 1 answered (floor exists: safe to 24 tput/27 rect, floor 28), condition 2 answered with the named verb 'herdr pane layout' and raw JSON output vendored in the doc, condition 3 answered numerically (half-width 111 ≥ 28 + margin 83); a suite test pins exactly-one-decision.
✓ Refusal is a first-class green outcome with a reachable gate branching on probe-ran-and-refused vs probe-could-not-run: The suite branches on resultsExist: absent → asserts '## Probe refused' heading in 43-MEASUREMENT.md with zero captures required (honest green exit for state B); present → the derivation/provenance/decision tests run, and the exactly-one-decision test accepts 'Grid refused' as a valid single heading for state A.
✓ IF LICENSED: exported pure decision function consuming the measured floor + runtime width, fail-closed to 'down', incident-e8aa003 geometry yields single-column pinned red-capable, herdr.ts law comment cites the measurement: splitDirection(currentCols, safeFloor) exported from herdr.ts; SAFE_FLOOR_COLS=28 with a comment citing 43-MEASUREMENT.md and the measured 24/27; undefined/NaN/Infinity/-1 → 'down' (fail closed, tested); incident geometry (2, 4, 50) → 'down' pinned in zero-token tests with a boundary pair (56→right, 55→down) that reddens on any weakening; the joinGroup law comment cites 43-MEASUREMENT.md as the licensing evidence.
✓ IF REFUSED: down-only splits, law comment extended, no-right pin: Not applicable — the measured outcome is LICENSED, and the stub-driver test still pins fail-closed 'down' whenever no real width is introspectable (no '--direction right' without a measured-safe width).
✓ The zero-token suite never runs the probe: script refuses without HERDR_ENV=1 + discovered CLI; tests only read vendored captures, exercise the pure function, and check stub command logs: measure-trailer-width.mjs dies loudly unless HERDR_ENV=1, a usable herdr binary, a workspace id, and an authed CLI are all present; no test imports or spawns it — the suite reads captures, calls splitDirection, and greps the stub command log.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit clean, coverage floors hold, runtime deps remain exactly picomatch/yaml/zod: The diff adds no package.json changes (probe uses node builtins only) and no token-spending test paths; summary reports 847 passed / tsc exit 0 / all 9 coverage floors held, with nothing in the diff contradicting those claims.

data.notes: Do not retry glm-5.2 a fourth time and do not wake a human — the work is already done, only the finish is failing. Attempt 3's transcript shows the exact acceptance-gate defect (criterion b: banner never asserted against the capture files) was fixed with raw.includes(data.cliVersion) + toMatch(/\d/), all 850 tests green, tsc exit 0; the worker then burned the window re-running the full suite + coverage despite two prior time-budget instructions — this channel demonstrably ignores pacing guidance, so a retry with the same notes will stall the same way. Reroute to a different, instruction-following CLI with a purely mechanical finisher brief: the worktree for P43-03 already contains the completed, verified fix — do NOT re-implement, re-measure, or re-run the full suite; inspect existing state, run only `npx vitest run tests/drivers/trailer-width.test.ts` as a sanity check, commit everything uncommitted, and emit the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer immediately. If the rerouted finisher also fails to produce a trailer, escalate to human.

✗ Threshold measured against a real agent TUI with raw per-width captures + results.json carrying real-parser verdicts, OR an honest '## Probe refused'; no src/ touched at probe stage: Captures, results.json, and real-parser verdicts exist, but the probe ran `claude -p ... --output-format text` (headless) in the pane — not the agent's interactive TUI. The interactive renderer's hard-wrapping is the exact failure mode VIS-09 asks about (the cursor hard-wrap regression, the e8aa003 incident), and headless output through a pane cannot exhibit it; the worker's own SUMMARY admits this ('re-probe if a vendor diverges' from 'this headless claude probe'). Neither branch of the criterion holds: not a TUI measurement, and not a refusal — the grid was licensed on the weaker probe.
✗ Every vendored verdict recomputed from raw bytes by the real parser; every capture carries forgery-resistant provenance (real tput cols + CLI banner); exactly one ruling with herdr.ts citing 43-MEASUREMENT.md: The 10 results.json-listed captures are recomputed and carry provenance, and herdr.ts cites 43-MEASUREMENT.md with exactly one ruling. But 8 additional vendored captures (cols-222-r*, cols-111-r*, cols-55-r*, cols-27-r*) contain ONLY echoed command text — no tput cols output, no CLI banner — violating 'every capture'. They are unreferenced by results.json so the suite never asserts them. Additionally, every r1 capture ends with the third `run-probe.sh` invocation producing NO trailer; r1's parseOk=true derives from repeat-0's residual trailer under the same shared nonce (the exact stale-trailer failure the repo just hardened against), so the '2/2 repeats' floor evidence is half stale.
✓ Verdicts derived not narrated: raw captures + results.json + zero-token tests/drivers/trailer-width.test.ts re-running the REAL parseWorkerResult from src/adapters/prompt.ts and asserting match: trailer-width.test.ts imports the real parseWorkerResult from src/adapters/prompt.ts, re-reads each results.json capture, and asserts recomputed.ok === e.parseOk; suite passes (841 tests) and is zero-token. Mechanically satisfied — though it only covers captures listed in results.json (see criterion 2).
✗ Provenance pinned inside the captures (tput cols line + CLI banner), asserted by the suite against the capture files: The suite asserts the cols integer and the cliVersion banner ('2.1.207 (Claude Code)', a real banner not just the command name) against capture bytes — but only for the 10 entries in results.json. Eight vendored capture files contain neither a tput output line nor a banner and are never asserted; 'every vendored capture contains (a) and (b)' is violated on the artifacts themselves.
✓ Exactly one decision section ('Grid licensed' or 'Grid refused') in 43-MEASUREMENT.md answering all three licensing conditions with measured numbers: Exactly one '## Grid licensed' heading; condition 1 answered with the measured table (floor 10 tput/13 rect), condition 2 with the named verb `herdr pane layout --pane <id>` plus raw JSON output (and the failed `pane get --help` attempt with raw output), condition 3 with 222→111 ≥ 10. The suite pins licensed+refused count === 1.
✓ Refusal is a first-class green outcome with a reachable gate branching on probe-ran-and-refused vs probe-could-not-run: trailer-width.test.ts branches on existsSync(results.json): absent → asserts '## Probe refused' in 43-MEASUREMENT.md with no captures required; present → capture-derivation path. Both branches reachable; shape (A) would flow through the same decision-section count test naming '## Grid refused'.
✓ IF LICENSED: exported pure splitDirection consuming the measured floor constant + runtime width, fail-closed to 'down', incident-e8aa003 geometry pinned to single-column, law comment cites the measurement: SAFE_FLOOR_COLS=10 (comment cites 43-MEASUREMENT.md), exported pure splitDirection() returns 'down' on undefined/NaN/Infinity/negative; suite pins splitDirection(2)→'down' (incident geometry) and the boundary; herdr.ts:184 comment extends the D-10 law citing the measurement; runtime width via paneWidth() with catch→undefined→'down'. Narrow-stub test (paneWidth:18) pins zero '--direction right'.
✓ IF REFUSED: down-only splits preserved with extended law comment and a no-right-split pin: Not applicable — the licensed branch was taken; the fail-closed narrow-width pin ('43-MEASUREMENT: narrow pane width → no rightward split') preserves the down-only behavior wherever width is insufficient or introspection fails.
✓ Zero-token suite never runs the probe; scripts/measure-trailer-width.mjs refuses without HERDR_ENV=1 + discovered agent CLI; tests only read vendored captures, exercise the pure function, and assert stub command logs: Grep confirms no test references measure-trailer-width; the script dies without HERDR_ENV=1, HERDR_WORKSPACE_ID, herdr, and a probed CLI; tests read vendored files and drive the bash stub only.
✓ npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit clean, coverage floors hold, runtime deps exactly picomatch/yaml/zod, probe uses node builtins only: Verified in the worktree: tsc exits 0; npm test 841 passed/1 skipped; npm run test:coverage exits 0 with floors held; package.json dependencies are exactly picomatch, yaml, zod; the probe imports only node:child_process/fs/path/url plus dist/adapters/prompt.js.

data.notes: glm-5.2 has now stalled twice on this task with the identical signature — completes the work, then burns the final window re-running the full suite instead of emitting the trailer — and the resume wiped the attempt counter, losing the earlier reroute decision. Do not dispatch pi:glm-5.2 again for P43-03 under any circumstances. Reroute to claude-code:sonnet (the escalation that was preempted by the resume), but NOT as a blind mechanical finisher: attempt 4's trailer-emitting run failed acceptance on substance (headless `claude -p` probe instead of the interactive TUI, 8 vendored captures with no tput/banner provenance, r1 verdicts contaminated by a stale repeat-0 trailer). Brief the new worker to first audit the current worktree's fresh glm work against those three specific acceptance failures — especially whether the probe drove a real interactive TUI and whether every vendored capture carries tput-cols + CLI-banner provenance asserted by the suite — fix only the concrete gaps found, run `npx vitest run tests/drivers/trailer-width.test.ts` plus one full `npm test` pass at most, commit, and emit TICKMARKR_RESULT immediately; no re-measurement unless the probe was again headless. If this attempt fails acceptance or stalls, go to human — three vendors will have failed the same criterion, which means the task or its acceptance bar needs re-planning, not another retry.

✓ Measured against real TUI with raw captures + machine-readable results.json (or honest Probe refused), no src/ touched at measurement stage: 8 raw per-width captures (108/53/25/24 cols × 2 repeats) against cursor-agent 2026.07.09-a3815c0 with results.json carrying per-width parse verdicts; the licensed ruling then touches src/ as criteria 2/7 mandate.
✓ Vendored verdicts recomputed by real parser; forgery-resistant provenance in captures; exactly one ruling with herdr.ts citing 43-MEASUREMENT.md: trailer-width.test.ts recomputes every verdict via the real parseWorkerResult and passes (verified by running the suite); captures carry PROBE_TPUT_COLS + cursor-agent banner; exactly one ruling (Grid licensed) and herdr.ts cites 43-MEASUREMENT.md at both the constant and the split site.
✓ Verdicts derived, not narrated — zero-token suite test re-runs real parseWorkerResult over each capture and asserts match to results.json: tests/drivers/trailer-width.test.ts imports parseWorkerResult from src/adapters/prompt.ts, re-reads each vendored capture, asserts parsed.ok === e.parseOk; test executed and passed (37/37 green).
✓ Provenance pinned inside captures (tput cols + CLI banner) and asserted against capture files: Every capture contains PROBE_TPUT_COLS=N and the cursor-agent version banner; the suite asserts both against the capture bytes (regex on PROBE_TPUT_COLS=${e.cols}, cliVersion substring, /cursor-agent|Cursor Agent/), not against the doc.
✓ Exactly one decision section (Grid licensed/refused) answering all three licensing conditions with measured numbers: Exactly one '## Grid licensed' heading (grep count 1) answering: (1) floor exists — narrowest safe 53, adopted 108; (2) introspection verb `herdr pane layout --pane <id>` → rect.width, with raw JSON output and negative results for pane get/tab get; (3) 222/2=111 ≥ 108+2=110.
✓ Refusal is a first-class green outcome with a reachable gate branching on the two refusal shapes: The gate branches on existsSync(results.json): captures present → derived-verdict + provenance + 'Grid (licensed|refused)' assertions (shape A covered); captures absent → asserts '## Probe refused' in 43-MEASUREMENT.md with zero captures required (shape B honest green exit).
✓ IF LICENSED: exported pure decision function on measured constant + runtime width, fail-closed to down, incident geometry pinned red-capable, herdr.ts law comment cites measurement: workerSplitDirection(paneCols, floor=108, margin=2) exported and pure; null/0/introspection-failure → 'down'; zero-token tests pin w∈{2,4,25,50,100,108}→down, 222→right, constants pinned at 108/2; stub tests (paneCols:100, layoutFails) redden on weakening; split-site comment cites 43-MEASUREMENT.md and e8aa003.
✓ IF REFUSED: down-only splits preserved with extended law comment and pinning test: Not applicable — the measurement licensed the grid; the refused branch's obligations are vacuously satisfied, and the down-only behavior below the floor remains pinned by the narrow-width and layout-failure tests.
✓ Zero-token suite never runs the probe; npm test zero-token, tsc --noEmit exits 0, coverage floors hold, runtime deps exactly picomatch/yaml/zod: Probe script dies without HERDR_ENV=1 + discovered CLI and no test invokes it (suite only reads vendored captures); verified live: tsc exit 0, targeted tests 37/37 pass, npm run test:coverage exit 0 with floors held, package.json dependencies unchanged (picomatch, yaml, zod), probe uses node builtins only.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Margin does not account for split chrome: gate licenses at source-pane 220 (110 ≥ 108+2) but the probe shows a right split of a 222-col pane yields ~108-col children (borders eat ~3 cols), so a 220-col pane's children land ~107 — still empirically safe (53 measured safe) but the stated margin is nominal, not physical
- Provenance test hardcodes /cursor-agent|Cursor Agent/i instead of deriving the banner check from results.json's cli field — a future probe re-run with a different CLI breaks the test even with valid captures
- Suite asserts /Grid (licensed|refused)/ appears but not the acceptance criterion's 'EXACTLY ONE decision section' (a doc with both headings would still pass)
- Passing captures contain the echoed prompt which itself parses as a valid trailer — parseOk=true could theoretically come from the echo rather than the agent reply; equivalent for width measurement (same renderer, same width) but slightly weakens the anti-fabrication story
